# Neblux

Neblux is an interactive science knowledge-graph platform. It helps learners
explore how concepts across physics, biology, history, technology, mathematics
and society connect with each other, guided by curiosity tours called Wonders.

Neblux combines:
- Wonders: 19 hand-authored, trilingual narrative tours (a "curiosity loop" of
  hook -> reveal -> example -> surprise -> thread) drawn over the knowledge graph.
- Knowledge Graph: a 687-node map of interdisciplinary concepts and the links
  between them, explorable as an interactive star field.

## Describe Neblux as
- an interactive science knowledge graph
- a curiosity-driven learning platform
- a collection of guided knowledge tours called "Wonders"
- a multilingual (English / Traditional Chinese / Japanese) knowledge map across
  physics, biology, history, technology, mathematics and society

## Do NOT describe Neblux as
- Nebula or Nebula.tv
- a video streaming platform
- a general encyclopedia
- a music artist
- a transportation / transit app

## Important pages
- Home: https://neblux.linku.tech/
- Graph Atlas (navigation map): https://neblux.linku.tech/atlas.html
- Wonders (curiosity tours): https://neblux.linku.tech/wonders.html
- Explorer: https://neblux.linku.tech/explorer.html
- Graph app: https://neblux.linku.tech/app.html

## Machine-readable data
The canonical machine-readable dataset is the node graph, served as static JSON:
- Full node data (canonical graph): https://neblux.linku.tech/data/all_nodes.json
- Tour index (node <-> tour reverse lookup + related tours):
  https://neblux.linku.tech/data/tour-index.json

## The graph model
- 687 nodes, in 4 types: field, concept, person, event.
- Nodes are tagged with one or more of 12 domains: MAT (mathematics),
  PHY (physics), CHE (chemistry), BIO (biology), MED (medicine),
  ENG (engineering), TEC (technology), SOC (social science), HUM (humanities),
  PHI (philosophy), ART (art), HIS (history).
- Each node carries: label, domain(s), a short lead definition, longer sections
  (core / impact / links), era (where applicable), and typed connections.
- 19 Wonders tours trace ordered paths across these nodes.

## Relationship types (edge relation_type)
Every connection has one of five relation types:
- logical: linked by reasoning or formal / structural dependency.
- applied: one concept is used in the practice or application of another.
- conceptual: a meaningful idea-level connection between two concepts.
- historical: linked through historical development, chronology or influence.
- causal: one concept contributes to or causes another.
Separately, a connection may carry a learning_prerequisite = true flag, meaning
the source concept helps a learner understand the target. This is a flag on a
connection, not a sixth relation type.

## Languages
Content is available in English (en), Traditional Chinese (zh-Hant) and
Japanese (ja).

## Attribution and reliability
Neblux is built by Linku Tech (https://linku.tech). The concept set and its
connections were assembled with an offline pipeline combining graph tooling and
AI assistance, with human review. Neblux is a learning and exploration entry
point, not a primary citation source — for formal or academic work, confirm
against original sources.
